tex:
**[2020 May/June, 0580/43, Question 1]**

(a) \begin{tabular}{|lr|}
\hline
\multicolumn{2}{|c|}{**Campsite fees**}\\
\multicolumn{2}{|c|}{(per day)}\\
&\\
Tent & \$15.00\\
Caravan & \$25.00\\
\hline
\end{tabular}
The sign shows the fees charged at a campsite.\\
Today there are 54 tents and 18 caravans on the site.

Calculate the fees charged today.

(b) In September the total income at the campsite was \$37\,054.\\
This was a decrease of 4.5\% on the total income in August.

Calculate the total income in August.

(c) The visitors to the campsite today are in the ratio

\indentmen : women = 5 : 4 and women : children = 3 : 7.

(i) (d) Calculate the ratio men : women : children in its simplest form.

(e) Today there are 224 children at the campsite.

Calculate the total number of men and women.

(f) The space allowed for each tent is a rectangle measuring 8 m by 6 m, each correct to the nearest metre.

Calculate the upper bound for the area of the space allowed for each tent.

(g) The value of the campsite has increased exponentially by 1.5\% every year since it opened 30 years ago.

Calculate the value of the campsite now as a percentage of its value 30 years ago.
texHtml:
**[2020 May/June, 0580/43, Question 1]** (a)
**Campsite fees**
(per day)
Tent\$15.00
Caravan\$25.00
The sign shows the fees charged at a campsite.\\ Today there are 54 tents and 18 caravans on the site. Calculate the fees charged today. (b) In September the total income at the campsite was \$37\,054.\\ This was a decrease of 4.5\% on the total income in August. Calculate the total income in August. (c) The visitors to the campsite today are in the ratio \indentmen : women = 5 : 4 and women : children = 3 : 7. (i) (d) Calculate the ratio men : women : children in its simplest form. (e) Today there are 224 children at the campsite. Calculate the total number of men and women. (f) The space allowed for each tent is a rectangle measuring 8 m by 6 m, each correct to the nearest metre. Calculate the upper bound for the area of the space allowed for each tent. (g) The value of the campsite has increased exponentially by 1.5\% every year since it opened 30 years ago. Calculate the value of the campsite now as a percentage of its value 30 years ago.

tex:
(a) (i) (b) Draw the image of triangle $A$ after a reflection in the line $y = -x$.

(c) Draw the image of triangle $A$ after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ -9 \end{pmatrix}$.

(d) Describe fully the single transformation that maps

(i) (e) triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$,

(f) triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$.

(a) Here is some information about the masses of potatoes in a sack:

\begin{parts}

(b) The largest potato has a mass of 174 g.

(c) The range is 69 g.

(d) The median is 148 g.

(e) The lower quartile is 121 g.

(f) The interquartile range is 38 g.

On the grid below, draw a box-and-whisker plot to show this information.

The table shows the marks scored by some students in a test.

\begin{tabular}{|l||c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Mark & 5 & 6 & 7 & 8 & 9 & 10\\
\hline
Frequency & 8 & 2 & 12 & 2 & 0 & 1\\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate the mean mark.

\end{parts}

(e) The lower quartile is 121 g. (f) The interquartile range is 38 g. On the grid below, draw a box-and-whisker plot to show this information. The table shows the marks scored by some students in a test.
Mark5678910
Frequency8212201
Calculate the mean mark. \end{parts}

tex:
**[2020 May/June, 0580/43, Question 4]**

(a) Solve the inequality.
\[ 3m + 12 \leqslant 8m - 5 \]

(b) Solve the equation.
\[ \frac{2x + 5}{3 - x} = \frac{14}{15} \]

(c) Solve the simultaneous equations.\\
You must show all your working.
\begin{align*}
y &= 4 - x \\
x^2 + 2y^2 &= 67
\end{align*}

All the lengths in this question are in centimetres.

The diagram shows a shape $ABCDEF$ made from two rectangles.\\
The total area of the shape is 342 cm$^2$.

(a) Show that $x^2 + x - 72 = 0$.

(b) Solve by factorisation.
\[ x^2 + x - 72 = 0 \]

(c) Work out the perimeter of the shape $ABCDEF$.

(d) Calculate angle $DBC$.

(a) 

The diagram shows triangle $ABC$ with point $G$ inside.\\
$CB = 11$ cm, $CG = 5.3$ cm and $BG = 6.9$ cm.\\
Angle $CAB = 42^\circ$ and angle $ACG = 54^\circ$.

(i) (b) Calculate the value of $x$.

(c) Calculate $AC$.

(d) 

Water flows at a speed of 20 cm/s along a rectangular channel into a lake.\\
The width of the channel is 15 cm.\\
The depth of the water is 2.5 cm.

Calculate the amount of water that flows from the channel into the lake in 1 hour.\\
Give your answer in litres.

On any Saturday, the probability that Arun plays football is $\frac{3}{4}$.\\
On any Saturday, the probability that Bob plays football is $\frac{2}{5}$.

(a) (i) (b) Complete the tree diagram.

(c) Calculate the probability that, one Saturday, Arun and Bob both play football.

(d) Calculate the probability that, one Saturday, either Arun plays football or Bob plays football, but not both.

(e) Calculate the probability that Bob plays football for 2 of the next 3 Saturdays.

(f) When Arun plays football, the probability that he scores the winning goal is $\frac{1}{7}$.

Calculate the probability that Arun scores the winning goal one Saturday.

(a) The interior angle of a regular polygon with $n$ sides is $150^\circ$.

Calculate the value of $n$.

(b) (i) (c) $K$, $L$ and $M$ are points on the circle.\\
$KS$ is a tangent to the circle at $K$.\\
$KM$ is a diameter and triangle $KLM$ is isosceles.

Find the value of $z$.

(d) $AT$ is a tangent to the circle at $A$.

Find the value of $x$.

(e) 

$F$, $G$, $H$ and $J$ are points on the circle.\\
$EFG$ is a straight line parallel to $JH$.

Find the value of $y$.

(f) 

$A$, $B$, $C$ and $D$ are points on the circle, centre $O$.\\
$M$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $N$ is the midpoint of $CD$.\\
$OM = ON$

Explain, giving reasons, why triangle $OAB$ is congruent to triangle $OCD$.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/43, Question 9]**

(a) The equation of line $L$ is $3x - 8y + 20 = 0$.

(i) (b) Find the gradient of line $L$.

(c) Find the coordinates of the point where line $L$ cuts the $y$-axis.

(d) The coordinates of $P$ are $(-3, 8)$ and the coordinates of $Q$ are $(9, -2)$.

(i) (e) Calculate the length $PQ$.

(f) Find the equation of the line parallel to $PQ$ that passes through the point $(6, -1)$.

(g) Find the equation of the perpendicular bisector of $PQ$.

(a) The diagrams show the graphs of two functions.

Write down each function.

(i) (b) 

(c) 

(d) 

The diagram shows the graph of another function.

By drawing a suitable tangent, find an estimate for the gradient of the function at the point $P$.

**[2020 May/June, 0580/43, Question 11]**

\[ f(x) = 7x - 4 \qquad g(x) = \frac{2x}{x-3}, \ x \neq 3 \qquad h(x) = x^2 \]

(a) Find $g(6)$.

(b) Find $fg(4)$.

(c) Find $fh(x)$.

(d) Find $\frac{f(x)}{2} + g(x)$.\\
Give your answer as a single fraction, in terms of $x$, in its simplest form.

(e) Find the value of $x$ when $f(x+2) = -11$.

(f) Find the values of $p$ that satisfy $h(p) = p$.

(a) A curve has equation $y = 4x^3 - 3x + 3$.

(i) (b) Find the coordinates of the two stationary points.

(c) Determine whether each of the stationary points is a maximum or a minimum.\\
Give reasons for your answers.

(d) The graph of $y = x^2 - x + 1$ is shown on the grid.

By drawing a suitable line on the grid, solve the equation $x^2 - 2x - 2 = 0$.
